1236	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1: the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition
1237	
1238	These are general `PresheafOfModules` statements (any `F : C ⥤ D`, ring presheaves
1239	`R, S`), not specific to schemes; they are project-local because Mathlib's pinned commit
1240	exposes neither `extendScalars` nor `pullback₀` at the presheaf-of-modules level. Both are
1241	realised as the left adjoint of an existing right adjoint: `pushforward₀ F R` is
1242	definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))` (because `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose,
1243	witnessed by Mathlib's `restrictScalars (𝟙 R)).Full := inferInstanceAs (𝟭 _).Full`), and
1244	`restrictScalars φ` is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`; since `pushforward _` is
1245	always a right adjoint, so are these two, and their left adjoints `pullback₀`/`extendScalars`
1246	exist. The decomposition `pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀` then follows from the
1247	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ` by
1248	uniqueness of left adjoints (`Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso`).
1249	
1250	Per blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition` (D1). **OFF-PATH (iter-243 pivot).** D1 is
1251	axiom-clean and self-contained, but it was the first brick of the ABANDONED general
1252	strong-monoidal pullback build (`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, route (e)); the active
1253	route is the loc-triv chart-chase D1'–D4' (§below), which does NOT consume `extendScalars`/
1254	`pullback₀`. Retained as a correct, reusable presheaf-level decomposition; do NOT extend it
1255	toward the general build (D2 scalar-strong / D3 topological-interchange are NOT pursued). -/
1256	
1257	section PullbackLanDecomposition
1258	
1259	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1260	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
1261	
1262	/-- `pushforward₀ F R` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))`
1263	(since `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose). Project-local; carries the existence of the
1264	topological inverse image `pullback₀`. -/
1265	private lemma pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1266	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀.{u} F R).IsRightAdjoint :=
1267	  inferInstanceAs (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))).IsRightAdjoint
1268	
1269	/-- `restrictScalars φ` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`.
1270	Project-local; carries the existence of the extension of scalars `extendScalars`. -/
1271	private lemma restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1272	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
1273	  inferInstanceAs
1274	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (F := 𝟭 C) (R := F.op ⋙ R) φ).IsRightAdjoint
1275	
1276	/-- **The topological inverse image `pullback₀ := (pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint`**, the
1277	left adjoint of the fixed-ring pushforward. On underlying presheaves it is the left Kan
1278	extension along `F.op`. Project-local (no `PresheafOfModules` inverse-image functor at the
1279	pin); the carrier of D3. -/
1280	noncomputable def pullback0 (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1281	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} R :=
1282	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1283	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint
1284	
1285	/-- **Extension of scalars `extendScalars φ := (restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint`**, the left
1286	adjoint of restriction of scalars along a morphism of ring presheaves. Project-local; the
1287	carrier of D2 (strong monoidal via `distribBaseChange`). -/
1288	noncomputable def extendScalars (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1289	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} S ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) :=
1290	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1291	  (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint
1292	
1293	/-- The adjunction `pullback₀ ⊣ pushforward₀`. -/
1294	noncomputable def pullback0Adjunction (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1295	    pullback0 F R ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R :=
1296	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1297	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R)
1298	
1299	/-- The adjunction `extendScalars φ ⊣ restrictScalars φ`. -/
1300	noncomputable def extendScalarsAdjunction (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1301	    extendScalars φ ⊣ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ :=
1302	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1303	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ)
1304	
1305	/-- **D1 — the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition** (blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition`).
1306	For `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` presenting a pushforward of presheaves of modules, the presheaf
1307	pullback factors as extension of scalars followed by the topological inverse image,
1308	`pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀`. This is the left-adjoint reversal of the
1309	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, obtained
1310	from `Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso` (uniqueness of left adjoints). Project-local. -/
1311	noncomputable def pullbackLanDecomposition (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1312	    PresheafOfModules.pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback0 F R :=
1313	  (Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
1314	    (extendScalarsAdjunction φ) (pullback0Adjunction F R)
1315	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
1316	    (Iso.refl (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ))).symm
1317	
1318	end PullbackLanDecomposition
1319	
1320	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1'–D4' loc-triv pullback–tensor comparison
1321	
1322	The locally-trivial-restricted upgrade of the oplax comparison map
1323	`pullbackTensorMap` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ⟶ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) to an isomorphism, blueprint
1324	§`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, sub-lemmas D1'–D4'. -/
1325	
1326	section LocTrivPullbackTensor
1327	
1328	/-- **The sheafified `⊗ₘ` of the two `pullbackValIso`s (piece 4 of `pullbackTensorMap`) is an
1329	iso.** Factored out as a top-level lemma so the `tensorIso (C := _ ⋙ forget₂)` elaboration mirrors
1330	the proven `tensorObjIsoOfIso` (it does not elaborate cleanly inside a tactic block carrying
1331	`extract_lets` locals). Project-local helper for `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta`. -/
1332	private lemma isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
1333	    (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
1334	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1335	      (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1336	        (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1337	        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
1338	        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom))) :=
1339	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1340	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1341	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1342	      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f M))
1343	      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f N)))).isIso_hom
1344	
1345	/-- **Reduction of `pullbackTensorMap` iso-ness to the sheafified presheaf δ.**
1346	
1347	`pullbackTensorMap f M N` is the four-fold composite
1348	`(sheafificationCompPullback).hom ≫ a_Y.map δ ≫ (sheafifyTensorUnitIso).hom ≫ a_Y.map (tensorHom …)`.
1349	Three of the four factors (`sheafificationCompPullback`, `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`,
1350	and the `tensorHom` of the two `pullbackValIso`s) are isomorphisms unconditionally;
1351	the only conditional factor is the sheafification `a_Y.map δ` of the presheaf-level
1352	oplax comparison `δ`. Hence `pullbackTensorMap f M N` is an iso whenever that
1353	sheafified δ is an iso. This isolates the SOLE remaining content (the sheafified δ)
1354	for D2' (unit pair) and D4' (chart-chase). Project-local. -/
1355	lemma isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
1356	    (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules)
1357	    (h : letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1358	          (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1359	          (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1360	        IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
1361	          (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1362	          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val))) :
1363	    IsIso (pullbackTensorMap f M N) := by
1364	  unfold pullbackTensorMap
1365	  extract_lets φ φ' a_Y
1366	  -- piece 2 (the sheafified δ) is the only conditional factor — supplied by `h`.
1367	  haveI hδ : IsIso (a_Y.map
1368	      (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') M.val N.val)) := h
1369	  -- pieces 1 and 3 are `Iso.hom`s; piece 4 is `a_Y.map` of the (iso) `⊗ₘ` of the two
1370	  -- `pullbackValIso`s, supplied by the factored top-level helper.
1371	  exact IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (IsIso.comp_isIso' hδ
1372	    (IsIso.comp_isIso' inferInstance (isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso f M N)))
1373	
1374	/-- **Converse of `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`.** A morphism of presheaves of modules whose
1375	image under the associated-sheaf functor is an isomorphism lies (on underlying additive presheaves)
1376	in the sheafification localizer `J.W`. This is the same morphism-property identity
1377	`PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` (the sheafification
1378	functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`) read backwards. Project-local:
1379	needed to feed the flatness-free whiskering lemmas `W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_W` from a sheafified
1380	iso (the D2' η-bridge route, below). -/
1381	private lemma W_of_isIso_sheafification {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
1382	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
1383	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
1384	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
1385	    {A B : _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
1386	    (hf : IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f)) :
1387	    J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f) := by
1388	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
1389	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
1390	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := hf
1391	  rw [← h] at h2
1392	  exact h2
1393	
1394	/-- **D2' δ-wrapping — the sheafified cotensorator on the unit pair is an iso, given the η-bridge.**
1395	
1396	The presheaf-level oplax unitality `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.left_unitality_hom` factors the
1397	cotensorator `δ (pullback φ') 𝟙_ 𝟙_` of the abstract presheaf pullback through the unit comparison
1398	`η (pullback φ')` (right-whiskered by `F.obj 𝟙_`) and the (iso) left unitor. Sheafifying, the
1399	unitor factor and the `F.map (λ_ 𝟙_)` factor are isomorphisms unconditionally; the whiskered
1400	`η`-factor `a_Y.map (η F ▷ F.obj 𝟙_)` is an iso whenever `a_Y.map (η F)` is — because a sheafified
1401	iso lies in `J.W` (`W_of_isIso_sheafification`), `J.W` is stable under right-whiskering
1402	(`W_whiskerRight_of_W`, flatness-free), and `J.W`-morphisms sheafify to isos
1403	(`isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`). Hence the sheafified `δ` on the unit pair is an iso, which is
1404	exactly the hypothesis the reduction brick `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` consumes
1405	on `M = N = 𝒪`. Project-local; the **δ-wrapping** half of D2' (`lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit`),
1406	self-contained modulo the η-bridge `IsIso (a_Y.map (η (pullback φ')))`. -/
1407	lemma isIso_sheafifyDelta_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
1408	    (h : letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1409	          (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1410	          (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1411	        IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1412	          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')))) :
1413	    letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1414	        (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1415	        (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1416	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1417	      (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')
1418	        (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).val (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).val)) := by
1419	  letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1420	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1421	      (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1422	  set a_Y := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
1423	  change IsIso (a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ') (𝟙_ _) (𝟙_ _)))
1424	  set F := PresheafOfModules.pullback φ' with hF
1425	  have hWη : (Opens.grothendieckTopology (Y : TopCat)).W
1426	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F)) :=
1427	    W_of_isIso_sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val) _ h
1428	  have hWw : (Opens.grothendieckTopology (Y : TopCat)).W
1429	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F ▷ F.obj (𝟙_ _))) :=
1430	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := Y.presheaf) _ _ hWη
1431	  haveI hIsoW : IsIso (a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F ▷ F.obj (𝟙_ _))) :=
1432	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hWw
1433	  haveI hIsoLam : IsIso (a_Y.map (λ_ (F.obj (𝟙_ _))).hom) := inferInstance
1434	  have hBC : IsIso (a_Y.map
1435	      (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F ▷ F.obj (𝟙_ _) ≫ (λ_ (F.obj (𝟙_ _))).hom)) := by
1436	    rw [Functor.map_comp]; infer_instance
1437	  haveI hD : IsIso (a_Y.map (F.map (λ_ (𝟙_ _)).hom)) := inferInstance
1438	  have hlu := Functor.OplaxMonoidal.left_unitality_hom F (𝟙_ _)
1439	  have key : a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ F (𝟙_ _) (𝟙_ _)) ≫
1440	      a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F ▷ F.obj (𝟙_ _) ≫ (λ_ (F.obj (𝟙_ _))).hom)
1441	      = a_Y.map (F.map (λ_ (𝟙_ _)).hom) := by
1442	    rw [← Functor.map_comp]; exact congrArg _ hlu
1443	  have heq : a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ F (𝟙_ _) (𝟙_ _))
1444	      = a_Y.map (F.map (λ_ (𝟙_ _)).hom) ≫
1445	        inv (a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F ▷ F.obj (𝟙_ _) ≫ (λ_ (F.obj (𝟙_ _))).hom)) := by
1446	    rw [← key, Category.assoc, IsIso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
1447	  rw [heq]; infer_instance
1448	
1449	/-- **D2' assembly — `pullbackTensorMap` on the unit pair is an iso, given the η-bridge.**
1450	Chains the δ-wrapping `isIso_sheafifyDelta_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta` into the reduction brick
1451	`isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta` (on `M = N = 𝒪`). This is the full statement of
1452	D2' (`lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit`) modulo the single remaining η-bridge hypothesis
1453	`IsIso (a_Y.map (η (pullback φ')))` (the sheafification-mate identification of the sheafified
1454	presheaf unit comparison with `pullbackUnitIso`, the unit-side analog of
1455	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`). Project-local. -/
1456	lemma isIso_pullbackTensorMap_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
1457	    (h : letI φ' : (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1458	          (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).op ⋙ (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1459	          (f.toRingCatSheafHom).hom
1460	        IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1461	          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')))) :
1462	    IsIso (pullbackTensorMap f (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1463	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := by
1464	  apply isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta
1465	  exact isIso_sheafifyDelta_unitPair_of_isIso_sheafifyEta f h

1	\chapter{Relative Picard sheaf --- \texttt{Scheme.Modules.tensorObj} substrate (A.1.c.SubT)}
2	\label{chap:Picard_TensorObjSubstrate}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean
4	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor.lean

8	\section{Motivation: the abelian-group-valued relative Picard sheaf}
9	\label{sec:tensorobj_motivation}
10	
11	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
12	\(k\). In Kleiman's framework (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor},
13	\cref{def:rel_pic_sharp}), the relative Picard functor
14	\[
15	  \Pic_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
16	  \qquad
17	  T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^* \Pic(T),
18	\]
19	is, by definition, abelian-group-valued. The abelian-group structure on each
20	fiber \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) comes from tensor product of line bundles
21	(Stacks tag 01CR / Hartshorne~II.\S 6), and the structure descends to the
22	quotient because \(\pi_T^*\) is a group homomorphism.
23	
24	% SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
25	% Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
26	% abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
27	% groups)
28	% (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
29	% SOURCE QUOTE:
30	% "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
31	%  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
32	%  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
33	% abelian groups defined by the formula
34	% 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
35	% \end{dfn}
36	%  [\dots]
37	% \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
38	%   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
39	% 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
40	%  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
41	% topologies  by
42	% 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
43	% 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
44	%  \end{dfn}"
45	\textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2, Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs.
46	The absolute and relative Picard functors take values in the category of
47	abelian groups; the relative functor is the quotient of one such group by
48	another, and the structure morphisms of the resulting presheaf are group
49	homomorphisms.}
50	
51	Concretely, the abelian-group law on \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the assignment
52	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr] :=
53	\bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
54	with neutral element the class of the structure sheaf
55	\(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) and inverse
56	\(- \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\), where
57	\(\mathcal{L}^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}}(\mathcal{L},
58	\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T})\) is the dual invertible sheaf. Pullback of
59	\(\mathcal{O}\)-modules along \(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) preserves tensor
60	products and the structure sheaf, so the image
61	\(\pi_T^* \Pic(T) \subset \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a subgroup, and the quotient
62	\(\Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)\) inherits a canonical abelian-group
63	structure. This is the content of
64	\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.
65	
66	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
67	infrastructural, and --- crucially --- much smaller than a full
68	monoidal-category construction. The group law lives on \emph{isomorphism
69	classes} of line bundles: each group axiom is a \emph{proposition} asserting
70	the existence of an isomorphism (\(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\)),
71	and a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it. In
72	particular the coherence data of a monoidal category --- the pentagon,
73	triangle and hexagon identities, and a fortiori any closed structure --- is
74	\emph{never consumed} by a group law on iso-classes. What the law genuinely
75	requires on the carrier is therefore only:
76	\begin{enumerate}
77	  \item a binary tensor-product operation
78	    \(\otimes\, : \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \times \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
79	                  \to \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), well-defined on
80	    isomorphism classes;
81	  \item the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as a designated unit object
82	    for \(\otimes\);
83	  \item the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
84	    (\(\exists N,\ M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)) together with the three
85	    monoidal coherence isomorphisms of \(\otimes_X\): associativity
86	    \((M \otimes N) \otimes P \cong M \otimes (N \otimes P)\), unit
87	    \(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes M \cong M\) (and on the right), and commutativity
88	    \(M \otimes N \cong N \otimes M\). The inverse is carried by the
89	    predicate, not by a separate lemma.
90	\end{enumerate}
91	At Mathlib's pinned commit one might be tempted to obtain (3) by instantiating a
92	\emph{full} symmetric monoidal category on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) and
93	reading the three isomorphisms off it. Two Mathlib facts make such a route
94	conceivable. First, the category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) of presheaves
95	of modules over a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) is already a (symmetric)
96	monoidal category in Mathlib (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\)),
97	with the sectionwise relative tensor \((M \otimes_R N)(U) = M(U) \otimes_{R(U)}
98	N(U)\); this applies verbatim to the \emph{varying} structure sheaf
99	\(R = \mathcal{O}_X\). Second, Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
100	(\(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)) takes a
101	monoidal category \(C\), a localization functor \(L : C \to D\) at a morphism
102	property \(W\), and an instance \(W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), and produces a
103	\emph{full} monoidal-category structure on the localization \(D\); sheafification
104	of presheaves of modules is the localization \(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the
105	locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\), so a full monoidal structure on
106	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) would follow once \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) were
107	supplied.
108	
109	This full-monoidal-category route is, however, \emph{not} the goal, and is not
110	pursued. The goal is the commutative \emph{group} on locally-trivial iso-classes
111	(à la \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\)), and that group law consumes only propositions, so
112	it never needs a coherent monoidal category --- neither the
113	pentagon/triangle/hexagon identities nor the \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance
114	that the \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) API requires. What it needs is exactly the
115	\emph{existence} of the three coherence isomorphisms of (3), and these are produced
116	directly: the associator by gluing canonical local isomorphisms
117	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, via the single linchpin
118	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), the unitors by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\)
119	pattern (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}), and the braiding likewise
120	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). With those three existence facts the group law
121	assembles \emph{by hand} the commutative group of \(\otimes\)-invertible
122	\(\otimes\)-iso-classes --- the Picard group
123	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) --- each group-axiom field fed a single
124	existence-of-isomorphism. This is the scheme-theoretic analogue of Mathlib's
125	fixed-ring \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\),
126	whose \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) is read off the \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent
127	monoidal category \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) that
128	Mathlib supplies over a fixed ring; the present construction cannot take that
129	\(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) route, since the coherent monoidal category on
130	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) for the varying structure sheaf is exactly what it
131	avoids. The
132	\(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible},
133	Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) idiom) carries each inverse existentially.
134	The \emph{full} \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) / \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) coherent
135	monoidal category is therefore not built for the group law and is off the critical
136	path; what the group law does still need from this circle of ideas is the
137	\emph{existence} of the associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), realized as a
138	sheafification transport whose single open left-whisker obligation is closed by the
139	stalk--tensor commutation (d.2). The stalk apparatus (d.1/d.2) is accordingly
140	\emph{on} the critical path, while the coherent monoidal category itself is not.
141	
142	\section{Mathlib API survey}
143	\label{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}
144	
145	The relevant pieces of the Mathlib API at the pinned commit
146	(\texttt{b80f227}) were originally assembled into a single \emph{realization route}
147	(route~(e)): obtaining a full monoidal structure on the substrate by instantiating
148	Mathlib's abstract localization-of-monoidal-categories API rather than
149	hand-assembling the associator. That route is \emph{superseded}. The group law on
150	iso-classes never consumes a coherent monoidal category, and the associator is
151	obtained by direct gluing through \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, so the
152	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance on which route~(e) hinges is never required.
153	The survey below is retained as a Mathlib-API reference and to record why the route
154	is vestigial; the four Mathlib facts are all verified against the on-disk Mathlib
155	source.
156	
157	\paragraph{(i) The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category.} Mathlib's
158	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal} constructs a
159	monoidal-category instance
160	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\) on
161	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,(R \circ \mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,
162	\mathtt{RingCat})\), with the sectionwise relative tensor
163	\[
164	  (M \otimes_R N)(U) \;\;=\;\; M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)
165	\]
166	% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct` /
167	% `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, in

184	together with associator, left/right unitors and braiding making it symmetric
